paper_title: A note on arithmetic progressions with restricted differences
summary: Problem 9 from arXiv:2605.13628 asks whether an arithmetic triangle removal lemma exists for the restricted-differences setting over $\mathbb{F}_q^n$, analogous to how the multicolor sum-free result was used to prove such a lemma in the unrestricted setting. The paper was submitted on 2026-05-13; no follow-up work addressing this problem was found in the indexed literature, consistent with its status as a brand-new open question.
search_queries: arithmetic triangle removal lemma restricted differences Conlon Fox Pham 2026
arithmetic triangle removal lemma restricted differences finite field
notes: Paper published the day before review (2026-05-13). No follow-up found in indexed literature. The natural context is the arithmetic triangle removal lemma in $\mathbb{F}_q^n$ (cf. Green's lemma and the tight bound of Fox–Tidor–Zhao), and the question asks for a restricted-differences variant extending Theorem 2 of the paper.
